title: casearia povedae
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Casearia povedae, which is the same species as Casearia sylvestris, has antiulcerogenic benefits, specifically in preventing stress-induced lesions and inhibiting gastric secretion.
side_effects: Casearia povedae can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea, due to its high concentration of tannins and alkaloids. Prolonged use may also lead to liver and kidney damage, as the plant's toxic compounds can accumulate in the body. In severe cases, improper use of Casearia povedae may result in respiratory problems, such as difficulty breathing or even respiratory failure. Additionally, it may interact with certain medications, exacerbating conditions like high blood pressure, heart disease, or diabetes.
